goal: `$ yaptap --prompt <name>` records, transcribes, then pipes the transcript through an ollama LLM with a user-selected prompt, streaming the result to stdout.

in_scope: CLI flags: --prompt, --prompt-file, --model, --llm-model, --list-prompts; binary-relative config/prompts/ resolution via std::env::current_exe()
Prompt TOML file format and validation
Bundled default prompts in config/prompts/ (TOML format)
yaptap --list-prompts: list available prompts from config/prompts/
Python llm.py subprocess: stdin transcript + prompt file → streamed ollama response
Rust: spawn llm.py with transcript on stdin, stream stdout to terminal via byte buffer
Phase 2 error handling and exit codes

tasks.title: Define `Args` struct with `clap::Parser` derive covering --prompt, --prompt-file, --model, --llm-model, --list-prompts fields; parse at start of `main()`
Emit `error: --prompt and --prompt-file are mutually exclusive` to stderr and exit 1 when both flags are given
Implement binary-relative prompts directory resolution: use std::env::current_exe() to get binary path, resolve config/prompts/ as a sibling directory
For --list-prompts: read all *.toml files from the prompts dir and parse the name and description fields from each file
For --list-prompts: print `Available prompts (config/prompts/):` header followed by alphabetically sorted `  <stem>  <description>` lines to stdout and exit 0
Print `error: prompts directory not found: config/prompts/` to stderr and exit 1 when the binary-relative config/prompts/ directory is absent
Implement --prompt <name> resolution: look up <name>.toml in <binary_dir>/config/prompts/; emit `error: prompt '<name>' not found in config/prompts/` to stderr and exit 1 on miss
Implement --prompt-file <path> resolution: validate file exists; emit `error: prompt file not found: <path>` to stderr and exit 1 on miss
Validate prompt TOML in Rust: on TOML parse failure emit `error: prompt file is not valid TOML: <path>` to stderr and exit 1
Validate prompt TOML in Rust: check name, description, system fields present; emit `error: prompt file invalid — missing field '<field>': <path>` to stderr and exit 1 on missing field
After successful transcription, when a prompt is selected, print `Thinking...` to stdout
Spawn `python3 src/core/llm.py --prompt-file <path> [--model <name>]` with transcript piped to subprocess stdin
Pass `--model <name>` to `python3 src/core/transcribe.py` subprocess when --model flag is given
Stream llm.py subprocess stdout to terminal in real time using a byte buffer (not line-by-line, to avoid buffering tokens until newline)
On non-zero llm.py exit, print `error: LLM generation failed — <subprocess stderr>` to stderr and exit 1
Without --prompt or --prompt-file, preserve phase 1 behaviour: print raw transcript to stdout and exit 0
Extend ctrlc handler to kill the llm.py subprocess and delete temp WAV file on SIGINT during LLM streaming, then exit 130
Add AI intent comment block for stream_response() purpose and invariants
Validate prompt_path is non-empty in stream_response() and raise ValueError otherwise
Validate prompt file exists in stream_response() and raise ValueError when missing
Parse prompt TOML in stream_response() using tomllib; raise ValueError on parse error
Validate 'system' field present in parsed TOML dict in stream_response(); raise ValueError if missing
Build ollama messages list: system message from prompt['system'], user message from transcript
Call ollama.chat(model, messages, stream=True), yield each token via chunk['message']['content'], and wrap ollama failures as RuntimeError
Implement main(): read transcript from stdin, parse --prompt-file and --model args, iterate stream_response(), print each chunk with flush=True, print final newline
Print `error: <message>` to stderr and exit 1 for ValueError and RuntimeError in main()
Create src/core/llm_test.py using Python unittest
Mock ollama.chat in all llm_test.py tests
Add test: valid transcript + prompt file yields non-empty token chunks
Add test: empty prompt_path raises ValueError
Add test: missing prompt file raises ValueError
Add test: invalid TOML in prompt file raises ValueError
Add test: missing 'system' field in prompt TOML raises ValueError
Add test: ollama.chat failure raises RuntimeError
Create config/prompts/email-reply.toml with name, description, system fields
Create config/prompts/meeting-notes.toml with name, description, system fields
Create config/prompts/slack-message.toml with name, description, system fields
Create config/prompts/action-items.toml with name, description, system fields
Create config/prompts/journal.toml with name, description, system fields
Verify `cargo install --path .` produces an installable yaptap binary with phase 2 flags
Verify end-to-end: yaptap --prompt email-reply records, transcribes, and streams LLM output
